summarization.a: The image depicts  the Volmer-Weber growth mechanism.
summarization.b: The image depicts the Frank-van der Merwe growth mechanism.
summarization.c: The image depicts the Stranski-Krastanov growth mechanism.

c.question: What is the Stranski-Krastanov growth mode mostly used for in nanoelectronics?
c.answer: c	This method is mostly used for the fabrication of quantum dots.

b.question: Does the Frank-van der Merwe growth mode result in good uniformity for thin films?
b.answer: b	This growth mode is extremely suitable for high uniformity for extremely thin films as the full substrate is covered already after a single cycle.

a.question: What is a different name for this type of growth?
a.answer: a	A different name of this growth mode is island growth, as the growth areas are islands of material.

a.question: Will the Volmer-Weber growth mode lead to fast uniform layers or not w.r.t. the other growth modes?
a.answer: a	This growth mode will not lead to fast uniform layers as the islands will first grow vertically.
